properties.name: Saya and the Dragon
properties.notes: Fantasy road-trip con story centered on Saya and an ancient dragon, with a more stable and consistent worldbuilding corpus than the Emily project.

properties.name: Saya
properties.role: protagonist / con artist / fake virgin sacrifice specialist
properties.notes: Beautiful, witty, promiscuous, low-born survivor from Seebulba. Smart, bratty, greedy, improvisational, not a trained fighter but dangerous in close chaos. Mispronounces complex words and survives through nerve, seduction, and stubbornness.

properties.name: The Dragon
properties.role: deuteragonist / ancient dragon / partner in crime
properties.notes: Ancient, sarcastic, gay, arthritic, sophisticated, fussy, hypochondriac dragon. Hoards treasure, fears aging and death, and reluctantly becomes Saya's closest ally and family-like companion.

properties.name: Saya and the Dragon
properties.notes: Starts as opportunistic collaboration around fake-sacrifice cons and becomes the emotional core of the story: banter, need, jealousy, protection, and chosen-family energy.

properties.name: The Inner Sea
properties.notes: Shared basin of civilization, piracy, trade, war, plague, fashion, and contact between cultures.

properties.name: Longland
properties.notes: Warm fertile southern shore of the Inner Sea; politically fragmented into rival city-states.

properties.name: The Redwall Mountains
properties.notes: Massive reddish mountain chain separating Longland from northern regions.

properties.name: The Baronies
properties.notes: Cold fragmented zone of petty violence north of Longland.

properties.name: Hanigalbat
properties.notes: Dry rocky warning-zone beyond the Baronies where civilization thins into survival.

properties.name: The Upper Steppe
properties.notes: Mobile power zone of steppe conflict and periodic catastrophe.

properties.name: The Wall (Ruins)
properties.notes: Collapsed former alliance wall marking collective failure rather than true border.

properties.name: Lycan Lands
properties.notes: Working wilderness of timber routes, hereditary lycans, class fracture, silver violence, and ideological cosplay versus survival reality.

properties.name: Tanagra
properties.notes: Ancient refined city at the meeting of the great river and the Redwall Mountains; politically important to Loma's background.

properties.name: Seebulba
properties.notes: Gritty mercantile hub central to Saya's past and survival story.

properties.name: The Longland City-States
properties.notes: Rival city-states whose cooperation is temporary, geographic, and self-interested.

properties.name: The Sisterhood of the Red Dawn
properties.notes: Militant ideological Amazon force north of Tanagra. Organized in banners under Virelda Sun-Clad. Locals call them the Scourge of the Upper Plains.

properties.name: Virelda Sun-Clad
properties.notes: Leader of the Sisterhood; charismatic tyrant claiming visions from the Mother Flame.

properties.name: Princess Loma of Tangara
properties.notes: Eldest surviving daughter of King Keldor and Queen Ilma. Legitimate heir by custom, but raised ornamental, politically naive, and unprepared.

properties.name: King Keldor of Tangara
properties.notes: Hedonistic father of Loma, obsessed with harem life and politically useless; dies through pox-conspiracy assassination.

properties.name: Queen Ilma
properties.notes: Legendary mother of Loma from the cursed House of Mara; destroyed Yadwiga's sanctuary and left behind myth, curse, and pressure.

properties.name: Princess Higara
properties.notes: Younger daughter of Keldor by another woman; politically lethal, terroristic, and the direct anti-Loma.

properties.name: The Pox Girl
properties.notes: Slave girl Thrixa used in the conspiracy that kills King Keldor via venereal plague.

properties.name: Axiarthanax
properties.notes: Sleeker younger dragon whose claim over Saya triggers jealousy and aerial conflict.

properties.name: Ogden
properties.role: recurring male figure tied to Loma and Saya
properties.notes: Associated with betrayal, romance-fragility, and sharp emotional fallout in later arc summaries.

properties.name: Sir Odran
properties.notes: Recurring knightly problem figure who disrupts scams, steals tribute, and chases the leads.

properties.name: The Cosmology
properties.content: Competing world-pictures involving flat-world or tray-world logic, giant snake foundations, layered heavens and hells, and multiple zodiac systems treated as lived belief rather than settled science.

properties.name: Road-trip con arc
properties.content: Recurring fake-sacrifice scam structure: Dragon terrorizes settlement, Saya infiltrates or poses as sacrifice, hero interference is manipulated, loot extracted, bond deepens.

properties.note: Core alliance and emotional axis.
Escaped whorehouse / survival background.
Southern shore.
One of the clearest large-scale threats to him.
Mix of awe, envy, respect, and fear.
Venereal plague assassination vector.
Recurring hero interference in scam pattern.

properties.name: The Dwarf
properties.notes: Drunken dwarf Saya repeatedly encounters in inns; occasional hazy sexual-history joke and low-level recurring familiarity.

properties.name: Gregory the Demon
properties.notes: On-and-off lover and trouble source from Saya's brothel-era past. Trickster energy, strong sexual chemistry, and the recurring goat debt joke.

properties.note: Brothel-times acquaintance and occasional lover.
Recurring inn-world encounter pattern.
Mutual cheating, attraction, and recurring adversarial chemistry.

properties.name: Sabrabena
properties.notes: Wealthier, softer, more perfumed estuary city in Longland.

properties.name: Delvida
properties.notes: More political and aristocratic estuary city opposite Sabrabena.

properties.name: Toemacha
properties.notes: Important port with somewhat divergent language and culture.

properties.name: Thelveth
properties.notes: Southern Longland city where the climate and temperament harden toward arid lands.

properties.name: Iolika
properties.notes: Inland administrative and trade center in Longland.

properties.name: Lerida
properties.notes: Strategic city controlling northern access to the Inner Sea; tied to counterfeit-silver issues in Lycan Lands material.

properties.name: Remnant Empire
properties.notes: Ancient shrinking empire across the Inner Sea, surviving on memory, ritual, grandeur, and mercenaries.

properties.name: Aurigena
properties.notes: Gold-Born capital of the Remnant Empire; overbuilt, magnificent, and politically stagnant.

properties.name: Beyond the Empire
properties.notes: Known to exist but poorly understood and intentionally undefined.

properties.name: Elven Kingdoms
properties.notes: Collection of remote elven polities with radically distinct atmospheres and practices.

properties.name: Velaromíel
properties.notes: The Grove; oldest kingdom, deeply forested, bound to tree-spirits older than time.

properties.name: Nyssalaithe
properties.notes: The Shroud; mist-hidden mountain realm of death rites and ghost-summoning.

properties.name: Ethuradien
properties.notes: The Glass Spire; salt-desert city of impossible towers, optics, divination, and mind-mirrors.

properties.name: Caldrithalen
properties.notes: The Bleeding Vale; crimson cliffs, waterfalls, skin-runes, and open-blood magic.

properties.name: Tirae Sanúr
properties.notes: The Silver Marches; tundra realm of giant white elk and moon-reflecting towers.

properties.name: Lunavaleth
properties.notes: The Petal Court; sealed caldera jungle realm ruled by queens.

properties.name: Orraquíneth
properties.notes: The Gloam; subterranean fungal forest kingdom, whispered and half-doubted.

properties.note: Ogden is the later/alternate name for Sir Odran, not a separate character.
Later-arc Ogden material belongs to the same character identity.
By later arc material, includes rivalry, blackmail, solidarity, emotional spillover, and temporary closeness.

properties.name: Tangara
properties.notes: Ancient austere Longland city at the mountain edge, functioning as Loma's family and palace center.

properties.name: House of Mara
properties.notes: Maternal line tied to Queen Ilma and Loma, carrying multi-generational divine curse through female heirs.

properties.name: Yadwiga
properties.notes: Divine figure whose sanctuary at Mupa was destroyed by Queen Ilma, leaving lingering mythic consequences.

properties.name: Mupa
properties.notes: Sanctuary of Yadwiga, destroyed in Ilma's campaign.

properties.name: Archduke Severin the Pale
properties.notes: Luxurious predatory aristocrat tied to Loma's later harem arc; offers status and enclosure, then reveals fangs beneath glamour.

properties.name: Saya and the Dragon
properties.notes: Relationship note capturing the stable emotional truth: opportunistic alliance becoming home, irritation, protection, and reliance.

properties.name: Saya and Sir Odran
properties.notes: Mutual fraud, attraction, humiliation, cheating, and recurring bad judgment. Covers Ogden material too.

properties.name: Saya and Loma
properties.notes: Mix of rivalry, pity, jealousy, blackmail, tenderness, shared damage, and unstable closeness.

properties.name: Loma and Ogden
properties.notes: Loma reaches toward him as possibility and is repaid with fragility, selfishness, and collapse.

properties.name: Dragon and the Sisterhood
properties.notes: The Sisterhood is one of the few forces the Dragon genuinely fears; they cast him as the Patriarch Serpent and know how to make him strategic.

properties.note: Canonical city identity for Loma's palace/dynastic background.
Inheritance through maternal line and curse burden.
Later harem/vampire arc false refuge.
Enters his household via Loma for luxury, revenge, and disruption.
Odran/Ogden unified identity.

properties.name: Goddess Ororo
properties.role: agrarian goddess / patron-judge of House of Mara
properties.notes: Minor but real goddess associated with black soil, plows, harvest, judgment, and the House of Mara line. Watches over Loma/Tangara material in some traditions and appears in judging visionary scenes.

properties.name: Old Rootbeard
properties.notes: Ancient ent-like authority figure leading angry tree-beings after the Dragon damages sacred woodland.

properties.name: The Blind Guardian
properties.notes: Ancient guardian of sacred waters whose death and consumption trigger Saya's contamination arc.

properties.name: Vaelthryx
properties.notes: Ancient dragon-name appearing both as the Dragon's old male fling and as an oath-level legendary reference in broader language.

properties.name: The First Mother
properties.role: foundational myth figure of the Sisterhood
properties.notes: Origin figure who melted her chains in fire and forged them into swords; ideological mother of the Sisterhood.

properties.name: Mother Flame
properties.content: Abstract purifying principle in Sisterhood belief: discipline, war, purification, rebirth through destruction.

properties.note: Some traditions frame Ororo as patron or judge of the Mara line.
Strong in visionary / WIP material around relic-shame and responsibility.
Triggered by woodland property damage after crashing into an ancient oak.
Not literally cosmology; used as nearest existing mythic/sacred note anchor for now pending a temple-arc node.
After temple encounter and consumption, develops humming and residual anomaly.

properties.name: Oleander of Gibonia
properties.notes: Refined, self-consciously sophisticated romantic/erotic figure who seduces through verse, performance, and literary softness more than brute confidence.

properties.name: Gibonia
properties.notes: Place-identity attached to Oleander; currently light world detail, stronger as a label than as a mapped region.

properties.note: Susceptible to his performed sophistication, songs, and literary seduction despite knowing better.
Treats him as polished nonsense and another example of Saya getting distracted by aesthetic men.
Normalized replacement for more voicey history labels; includes rivalry, pity, jealousy, blackmail, tenderness, and unstable closeness.
Normalized replacement for vendetta-tension label; mutual fraud, attraction, humiliation, and rivalry.
Normalized replacement for brief enchantment label; attraction mediated through literary performance and sophistication.
Normalized replacement for contemptuous_of label.
Canonical origin; supersedes stray Tanagra spelling drift.
Earlier spelling/name drift reconciled to Tangara as canonical form in current vault and ontology.
Duplicate relationship nodes reconciled; use rel_saya_dragon_named as the cleaner current note-backed form.
Inverse-normalized helper for query convenience; House of Mara is under Ororo's watch in some traditions.
Normalized replacement for complicates; keeps emotional and plot entanglement without vague wording.
Partial normalization of fascinated_by; awe/fear remains in note text but attraction to the image/power is real.
Canonical geography relation replacing Tanagra spelling drift.
Normalized directional helper replacing northwest_of for simpler querying.

properties.name: Aunt Threxaval the Bone-Eater
properties.role: draconic elder / terrifying aunt / ancestral authority
properties.notes: Ancient severe dragon elder associated with blood rites, excommunication, family judgment, and bone-throne-level ceremonial terror.

properties.name: Varnithax
properties.notes: Dead uncle haunting the Dragon's lineage with ultimatums, shame, worthiness tests, and cyclical deadline theatrics.

properties.note: Feared aunt and draconic elder authority.
Dead uncle / ghostly lineage judge.
Demands proof of worthiness to hoard and bloodline.
Embodies family-scale ritual judgment and exclusion.
Both are part of the Dragon's terrifying ancestral network.

properties.name: Hedge Wizard
properties.role: recurring minor magic-user / local occult figure
properties.notes: Secondary wizard figure in the wider road-world: provincial, magical, and useful as part of the setting's low-to-mid-tier sorcery ecosystem rather than grand cosmological authority.

properties.name: Swamp Hag
properties.role: recurring witch / hag / dangerous practical magic authority
properties.notes: Swamp-based occult figure tied to curses, remedies, body-problems, and chaotic but often useful intervention. Functions as one of the story's recurring practical-witch authorities.

properties.note: Turns to the hag for curse-work, remedies, and filthy practical magic help.
Recurring road-world magic-user contact / secondary arc figure.
Frequently acts as filthy practical magical authority, diagnosing, mocking, and correcting Saya's messes.
Loose anchor for practical curse/remedy magic until a dedicated witchcraft or body-horror arc node exists.
Provincial road-world magic-user rather than imperial or temple authority.

properties.name: Lombasa
properties.notes: Grimy coastal market city associated with harbor commerce, brothels, alchemists, and quick monetization of exotic vice goods. In frog-arc material, Saya sells hallucinogenic frogs here to buyers and rival brothels.

properties.name: Urveth
properties.notes: City identified through the cursed Library of Urveth. Strongly associated with demonic scholarship, clerical authority, forbidden texts, and psychic danger rather than civic detail.

properties.name: Garmor the Well Hung
properties.role: WIP legendary hero / rumor-object of Saya's curiosity
properties.notes: Recent mega-arc figure introduced through tavern legend. Framed as a near-mythic heroic himbo with heavy sexual reputation. Current status is intentionally soft and WIP, but Saya becomes immediately fascinated and starts pursuing the rumor under fake 'scholarship' excuses.

properties.note: Shared rough port/vice texture in current material.
Coastal rough-port family resemblance; Thelveth docks are mentioned in the same rat/coast texture.
In Lore 2 frog material, she converts hallucinogenic frogs into cash in Lombasa's harbor and brothel economy.
She frees the Succubus from sexual slavery in the cursed Library of Urveth.
Recent mega-arc WIP: she hears the tavern legend and immediately starts pursuing him under fake anthropological pretenses.
His rumor-profile lands exactly in Saya's weakness zone: impressive heroics, tavern legend, and sexual bravado.
Supported by frog-selling / brothel-vice material in Lore 2.
Supported by the Library of Urveth arc and Succubus backstory.
Track as active WIP legend rather than fully stabilized recurring character.

properties.name: Rugrun the Gruff
properties.role: ancestral founder-hero / House Mara legitimacy anchor
properties.notes: Legendary founder figure of House Mara. In recent Iolica/Iolika heist material, his sacred nose ring functions as a proof-of-lineage relic for Loma's dynastic claim. He is remembered as large enough that the ceremonial ring is absurdly huge, more like monumental bronze hardware than ordinary jewelry.

properties.title: Sacred nose ring of Rugrun the Gruff
properties.summary: Ceremonial bronze lineage-relic of House Mara tied to Rugrun the Gruff. In the Iolica/Iolika heist material it proves comically enormous—cart-wheel scale—and becomes the target of Saya and Loma's failed heist before being lost in the river.
properties.path: C:\Users\Big Dell\Documents\Saya and the Dragon\01 Characters\Rugrun the Gruff.md

properties.note: Recent WIP lineage material identifies him as founder of House Mara.
Loma believes possession of the relic can affirm her dynastic claim.
Lineage relic / proof object for Mara succession politics.
In the Iolica/Iolika heist arc, she and Loma recover it from a vault before losing it in the river.
Treats it as her last patrimony / inheritance proof.
Nose-ring heist creates scam-partner farce, dynastic panic, and shared disaster.
Ancestral legend stabilized via the Iolica/Iolika relic-heist material.

properties.name: The Aethrafax Brothers
properties.role: conjoined rival-dragon brothers / published arc threat
properties.notes: Two-headed black dragon pair sharing one body. One head is spectacularly stupid, gluttonous, and easy to manipulate; the other is highly intelligent, strategic, and enraged at permanent fusion to an idiot. Killed in the altar-village arc after Saya is used as bait and the Dragon crushes the smarter head with a boulder.

properties.name: The Hermit of the Karma Hill
properties.notes: Cranky desert-hill hermit consulted by Saya in the bad-karma arc. Mocking, misogynistic, unhelpful, and occasionally truthful. Associated with a foul-mouthed crow and with vague suggestions that Saya may indeed be carrying some old curse.

properties.name: The Oracle of Holy Embroidery
properties.notes: Spiritual encounter-authority from the karma arc. Associated with holy embroidery aesthetics and golden llamas. Accuses Saya of devouring a sacred Mogwai, then banishes her without useful help, deepening the arc's panic and absurdity.

properties.note: Final kill in the published altar-village arc lands via boulder strike after bait-and-moonshine chaos.
She is used as bait in their lair and nearly becomes sacrifice-consumption collateral.
Climbs the hill in the bad-karma arc seeking curse / fate clarity.
Mocks her, half-reads her, and suggests some old curse may indeed be in play.
Crow joins the Hermit in ridiculing Saya during the karma consultation.
Accuses her of devouring a sacred Mogwai and banishes her.
Part of the bad-karma / Mogwai panic sequence.
Actually published-arc stable; relation label retained for lightweight graph compatibility.
Stable karma-arc spiritual figure; using existing lightweight project relation family.
Stable karma-arc spiritual figure; using existing lightweight project relation family.

properties.name: Madam Zoobaya
properties.notes: Luxury-facing pleasure-house madam who shaped Saya's past. Markets chains, punishments, themes, correction, and eroticized discipline as refinement or empowerment. Makes coercion look desirable and profitable; part of a larger harbor debt/crime ecosystem.

properties.name: Temple of the Bleeding Heart
properties.notes: Seebulban religious-commercial institution that receives girls through court order, guard sweeps, or indenture and sorts them into ornamental or labor tracks. Spiritual language, branding, ritual, discipline, and sexual commerce are fused into one system.

properties.name: Madam Zoobaya's Pleasure House
properties.kind: luxury pleasure house / coercive erotic complex
properties.notes: Associated naming variants include House of Eternal Moans, House of Eternal Delights, House of Silk, and Emporium of Educated Delight. Velvet, baths, chains, themes, punishments, glitter, and luxury surfaces masking sale, coercion, and debt.

properties.name: Looxa
properties.role: secondary house girl / Zoobaya-house social rival
properties.notes: Appears in Zoobaya-house material around the honey-cake accusation sequence. Helps show how collective punishment breeds betrayal inside the house.

properties.name: Gawa
properties.notes: Appears in scraps material as another worker Saya meets. Signals low-class solidarity, bruised competence, and lateral recognition within sex-work survival space.

properties.note: Zoobaya is one of the major human-scale backstory forces in Saya's formation.
Temple system fused piety, punishment, sexual training, and indenture into her developmental survival curriculum.
Remembers the house as materially seductive and structurally coercive.
Later material links Loma to fallen-princess themed exploitation inside Zoobaya's system.
Street/worker-level recognition and camaraderie in scraps material.
Important backstory and later-Loma exploitation site.
Actually foundational/stable; relation family reused for graph compatibility.
Secondary house figure from scraps / house scenes.
Secondary street/tavern worker figure from scraps.
Foundational institution in Saya's backstory.

properties.name: Material Culture of the Inner Sea World
properties.content: Reference node for the setting's lived material culture: food, clothing, ornament, vessels, baths, incense, trade goods, and the overlap between luxury, ritual, vice, and coercion.

properties.name: Inner Sea food culture
properties.content: Recurring food texture includes seafood, goat and sheep products, bread and flatbread, olives, chickpeas, onions, peppers, figs, nuts, honey, wine, rough ale, pastries, and regional specialties such as pumpkin products in the Redwall zone.

properties.name: Longland clothing and adornment
properties.content: Baseline clothing system centers on linen tunics, silk wraps, wool cloaks, sandals or bare feet, bronze fittings, veils, belts, brooches, chains, anklets, toe rings, bangles, perfume, dyes, body paint, and presentation as class or role technology.

properties.name: Luxury, ritual, and coercion overlap
properties.content: Temples, brothels, dynastic houses, and pleasure complexes share a material vocabulary of veils, incense, chains, baths, velvet, mirrors, attendants, ritual display, and bodily control. Beauty often functions as infrastructure.

properties.note: Worldbuilding note tracking lived texture rather than only places and characters.
Strongest dockside seafood and rough harbor-food texture.
Pastries, plum wine, flirtation, and aestheticized dining culture.
Goat cheese, onions, beans, squash, and hardy inn food.
Uniform austerity, banner colors, shaved heads, linen drill wear, bronze insignia, and barefoot discipline.
Core institution where ritual, beauty, punishment, and sexual commerce fuse.
Luxury pleasure-house version of the same coercive material vocabulary.
City-state baseline of linen, silk, wool, sandals, bare feet, bronze, and ornament.

properties.name: Tanagra
properties.notes: Canonical spelling. Ancient refined Longland city-state at the meeting of the great river and the Redwall Mountains; politically central to Loma, Keldor, Ilma, Higara, House of Mara, and Ororo material.

properties.name: Tangara
properties.notes: Legacy spelling retained only for historical graph continuity. Canonical current spelling is Tanagra.

properties.name: Princess Loma of Tanagra
properties.notes: Canonical spelling updated to Tanagra. Eldest surviving daughter of King Keldor and Queen Ilma; legitimate heir by custom, raised ornamental and politically unprepared.

properties.name: King Keldor of Tanagra
properties.notes: Canonical spelling updated to Tanagra. Hedonistic ruler whose negligence helps trigger the palace succession catastrophe.

properties.name: Iolica
properties.notes: Canonical spelling updated to Iolica. Inland Longland administrative and trade-routing center; useful counterweight to the port-city texture.

properties.title: Sacred nose ring of Rugrun the Gruff
properties.summary: Ceremonial bronze lineage-relic of House Mara tied to Rugrun the Gruff. In the Iolica heist material it proves comically enormous—cart-wheel scale—and becomes the target of Saya and Loma's failed heist before being lost in the river.
properties.path: C:\Users\Big Dell\Documents\Saya and the Dragon\01 Characters\Rugrun the Gruff.md

properties.name: Spice trade and spice culture
properties.content: Spices function as trade goods, class markers, market identity, vice-economy texture, and caravan/shipborne wealth. Repeated references include spice carts, spice stands, spice quarters, spice ships, spice syndicates, spice sacks, spice merchants, spiced chickpeas, spiced yogurt, spiced wine, spiced tea, and Delvida's spice-heavy cuisine. Seebulba is even framed in one chapter voice as Mistress of Spice and Sin.

properties.note: Canonical spelling now runs Tanagra; keep Tangara only as legacy alias for historical drift.
Canonical origin spelling.
Canonical city spelling.
Canonical city spelling.
Canonical city spelling.
Spice references recur often enough to deserve a dedicated setting note.
Harbor identity, spice markets, and the Mistress of Spice and Sin phrasing.
Dragon explicitly frames Delvidan cuisine as spice-forward and marinated.
Iolica market scenes include spice carts, shared dishes, and trade-display texture.

properties.name: Spice trade and intoxicants
properties.content: Tracks recurring spice-market texture plus named substances: jalbaljah, ilanta leaves, gigglegrass, and heatgrass. Spices function as trade goods, class signals, cuisine markers, and vice-economy texture; some botanicals overlap with smoke blends and aphrodisiacs.

properties.name: Jalbaljah
properties.content: Named spice. The Dragon explicitly calls it aggressive even by dragon standards, implying an intensely hot or forceful spice used by humans in cuisine or blends.

properties.name: Ilanta leaves
properties.content: Flexible leaves used to roll smoke blends. Saya says ilanta leaves roll best when warm; skin heat and oils help keep them from cracking.

properties.name: Gigglegrass
properties.content: Recreational smoke blend / intoxicant. Saya steals a gigglegrass pouch, later rolls giggle grass in ilanta leaves, and smokes it socially.

properties.name: Heatgrass
properties.content: Dried botanical aphrodisiac or desire-amplifier. Specifically identified as amplifying suppressed desire rather than creating new desire. Velurian heatgrass is a named variety in the convent-aqueduct sabotage chapter.

properties.note: Named spice and botanical references are now stable enough to track directly.
Fits culinary/aromatic spice logic.
Ilanta leaves are the rolling medium for gigglegrass.
Rolled in ilanta leaves in scraps material.

properties.name: Chicka-chicka
properties.content: Named spice or seasoning from the Diving for Seafood chapter. Saya presents it as even more intense than jalbaljah; the Dragon says it is worse and should probably be regulated by priests. Children are said to eat it in Seebulban/coastal culture.

properties.name: Gingha leaves
properties.content: Leaves chewed after very hot food. Saya says they soothe the belly after harsh spices; they also turn the tongue purple, which she treats as a tavern flirtation trick.

properties.note: Part of the named hot-spice layer in coastal cuisine.
Post-spice digestive/soothing leaf with tavern-social use.
Same conversation; chicka-chicka is framed as even harsher than jalbaljah.
Chewed afterward to soothe the belly after extreme spice.
Saya frames this spice tolerance as normal enough that Seebulban children eat it.
Purple-tongue gingha use is described as a Seebulba tavern trick.

properties.name: Madrigal
properties.kind: port city / siege city / mercantile harbor
properties.notes: Recurring port city with ancient sewers, harbor chains, fish-market and dungeon infrastructure, and a distinctly mercantile ethos of contracts and coin rather than holiness. Site of Loma's imprisonment, the clay warehouse discovery, and the later siege/recovery material.

properties.name: House of the Veiled Ember
properties.notes: Cold-storage disciplinary house on chalk cliffs above the Veil in the northern Baronies. Charcoal linen, enforced silence, renamed novices, barefoot frost, and punitive containment of inconvenient women; Loma is held here as “Novice Third Row.”

properties.name: Claymancy
properties.notes: Forbidden art of binding something living or soul-like into clay constructs. Distinguished by the Dragon from ordinary pottery and treated as scalable, unnatural, and ethically horrifying.

properties.name: Dragon blood
properties.notes: Illicit alchemical product derived from harvested dragon suffering and sold as potent lust-enhancing substance. The Dragon describes it as carrying dangerous echoes of power, hunger, memory, and madness beyond ordinary aphrodisiacs.

properties.name: The Succubus
properties.notes: Recurring infernal figure tied to Saya's past and to Gregory the Demon. Flirtatious, transactional, and operationally competent; disrupts the House of the Veiled Ember and later reappears as an active agent rather than a one-off note.

Beautiful, witty, promiscuous, low-born survivor from Seebulba. Smart, bratty, greedy, improvisational, not a trained fighter but dangerous in close chaos. Mispronounces complex words and survives through nerve, seduction, and stubbornness. In later material she is directly pressured by Ororo through judging dream-visions around Loma, repeatedly acts as Loma's rescuer despite denial, and crosses a moral line in the dragon-blood arc by considering the Dragon as a source of profit and power before trying to repair the breach.

Ancient, sarcastic, gay, arthritic, sophisticated, fussy, hypochondriac dragon. Hoards treasure, fears aging and death, and reluctantly becomes Saya's closest ally and family-like companion. Later material stresses his horror at claymancy, his knowledge of dragon-blood harvesting as institutionalized agony, and plot-relevant physical frailty including arthritic pain and wing-joint stiffness; he temporarily ruptures with Saya over blood betrayal before reconciling.

properties.kind: co-dependent partnership / road-trip scam alliance / chosen-family bond under strain
properties.notes: Starts as opportunistic collaboration around fake-sacrifice cons and becomes the emotional core of the story: banter, need, jealousy, protection, and chosen-family energy. Later arcs add a major rupture when Saya is willing to instrumentalize the Dragon's body through dragon-blood curiosity, followed by explicit apology, separation, and reconciliation rather than a simple reset.

Canonical spelling updated to Tanagra. Eldest surviving daughter of King Keldor and Queen Ilma; legitimate heir by custom, raised ornamental and politically unprepared. Later material has her imprisoned in Madrigal and then confined at the House of the Veiled Ember, stripped of name and status as “Novice Third Row,” reinforcing how often others try to store, trade, or manage her politically.

Minor but real goddess associated with black soil, plows, harvest, judgment, and the House of Mara line. Watches over Loma/Tangara material in some traditions and appears in judging visionary scenes. Later material makes her more direct and accusatory toward Saya, pressing her over abandonment and responsibility.

Luxury-facing pleasure-house madam who shaped Saya's past. Markets chains, punishments, themes, correction, and eroticized discipline as refinement or empowerment. Makes coercion look desirable and profitable; part of a larger harbor debt/crime ecosystem. Scrap material sharpens the backstory into a toxic but mutually impressed dynamic and explicitly has Zoobaya gamble Saya away toward the Iolikan slave-market pipeline.

Associated with betrayal, romance-fragility, opportunism, and sharp emotional fallout in later arc summaries. Chapters from the Madrigal / siege material reinforce him as the on-page name most commonly used for the recurring man tied to Loma and Saya, including participation in escape material.

Recurring knightly / romantic complication figure. Current material continues to support treating Sir Odran and Ogden as the same recurring man via aliasing even when the surface-name usage skews strongly toward Ogden in later chapters.
Younger daughter of Keldor by another woman; politically lethal, terroristic, and the direct anti-Loma. Later material links her more concretely to claymancy and associated atrocities.

properties.note: Chapters 371+ and siege material make Madrigal a recurring, stable setting.
Lore 2 makes the House of the Veiled Ember a distinct recurring location.
Claymancy becomes a major late-arc horror concept.

properties.notes: Cold-storage disciplinary house on chalk cliffs above the Veil in the northern Baronies. Charcoal linen, enforced silence, renamed novices, barefoot frost, and punitive containment of inconvenient women; Loma is held here as 'Novice Third Row'.

Canonical spelling updated to Tanagra. Eldest surviving daughter of King Keldor and Queen Ilma; legitimate heir by custom, raised ornamental and politically unprepared. Later material has her imprisoned in Madrigal and then confined at the House of the Veiled Ember, stripped of name and status as 'Novice Third Row', reinforcing how often others try to store, trade, or manage her politically.

properties.name: Bollo
properties.role: Taurean former regular / remembered lover from Saya's brothel years
properties.notes: Taurean brute strongly associated with Saya's genuinely fond sexual memory rather than coercive trauma. Remembered as huge, simple, earthy, physically satisfying, and emotionally uncomplicated; later material indicates a nostalgic reunion, while dream and masturbation material shows he remains one of Saya's persistent benchmarks for remembered bodily pleasure.

properties.name: Adora of Valhrona
properties.notes: Currently supported only by WIP index material. Adora of Valhrona is identified as holding Loma in a dungeon while Higara of Tanagra wants Loma alive for separate political purposes. Treat as real but highly provisional until chapter text is available.

properties.name: Valhrona
properties.notes: Currently supported only by WIP index material via “Adora of Valhrona.” Added conservatively as a place-name anchor; keep details soft until actual chapter text stabilizes it.

Legendary founder figure of House Mara. In recent Iolica/Iolika heist material, his sacred nose ring functions as a proof-of-lineage relic for Loma's dynastic claim. He is remembered as large enough that the ceremonial ring is absurdly huge, more like monumental bronze hardware than ordinary jewelry. He matters less as intimate character than as ancestral legitimacy-engine, showing how dynastic proof in this world depends on theatrical relics and bureaucratic absurdity.

Recent mega-arc / WIP figure introduced through tavern and market legend. Framed as a near-mythic heroic himbo with heavy sexual reputation: broad, confident, generous, and socially real as rumor before he is fully real as biography. Saya becomes immediately fascinated and pursues him under fake scholarly excuses; later material includes an actual encounter rather than rumor alone, but he should still be treated as somewhat soft/WIP compared with core recurring cast.

Luxury-facing pleasure-house madam who shaped Saya's past. Markets chains, punishments, themes, correction, and eroticized discipline as refinement or empowerment. Makes coercion look desirable and profitable; part of a larger harbor debt/crime ecosystem. Scrap and overview material sharpen the backstory further: she knows vice goods, punishes unauthorized use, can move girls through gambling and debt-transfer, and forms a toxic but mutually charged shaping relationship with Saya.

Secondary Zoobaya-house girl remembered through accusation and rivalry scenes inside the house's punishment economy. Observant, reactive, and willing to accuse when collective punishment turns girls against one another. She matters mainly as evidence of how Zoobaya's system converts shared vulnerability into competition and betrayal.

Secondary worker figure from tavern / street-sex-work scraps. Pragmatic, bruised, sharp, and grounded; produces immediate worker-to-worker recognition with Saya. Useful as texture for the world's low-class solidarity layer rather than as a major recurring independent plot mover.

properties.note: Bollo is a stable remembered backstory figure even if he is not a major active plot driver.
Repeated fantasy/dream material shows Bollo persisting as a benchmark of remembered pleasure.
She recalls him fondly as a former regular from brothel years.

Currently supported only by WIP index material via 'Adora of Valhrona.' Treat Valhrona as the current canonical spelling; 'Velhrona' should be treated as a likely variant or mistaken form until chapter text stabilizes the name.

properties.canonical_name_note: Alias of Ogden; later material favors the Ogden surface name while preserving same-character identity.
